title: Oracle Enterprise Manager Target
description: Schema for a monitored target in Oracle Enterprise Manager Cloud Control. A target represents any managed entity such as an Oracle Database, host server, middleware instance, application, or composite system monitored through the Enterprise Manager platform.

targetId.description: Unique identifier assigned by Enterprise Manager when the target is registered.

targetName.description: Display name of the target. Must be unique within the combination of target name and target type.

targetType.description: The type of the target, defining its monitoring template, available metrics, and configuration properties.

targetDisplayType.description: Human-readable display name for the target type.

hostName.description: Fully qualified hostname or IP address where the target resides.

lifecycleStatus.description: Current lifecycle management status of the target within Enterprise Manager.

availabilityStatus.description: Current availability status as reported by the monitoring agent. Indicates whether the target is reachable and operational.

agentUrl.description: URL of the Oracle Management Agent responsible for monitoring this target.

oracleHome.description: Oracle home directory path on the target host. Applicable to Oracle software targets such as databases and middleware.

machineName.description: Machine name where the target is deployed, which may differ from the hostname.

installLocation.description: Installation directory path for the target software.

timezone.description: Time zone of the target in standard timezone identifier format.

owner.description: Enterprise Manager username that owns this target and is responsible for its management.

timeCreated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the target was first added to Enterprise Manager.

timeUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent target configuration change.

properties.description: Target-type-specific configuration and instance properties. The available properties vary based on the target type. Common examples include SID, port, service name, and version.

SID.description: Oracle System Identifier for database targets.

OracleHome.description: Oracle home path as a target instance property.

MachineName.description: Machine name as a target instance property.

RACOption.description: Whether RAC is enabled for a database target.

RoleSeparated.description: Whether the Oracle installation uses role separation.

globalProperties.description: Global target properties used for classification, grouping, and organizational metadata.

orcl_gtp_line_of_bus.description: Line of business associated with the target.

orcl_gtp_cost_center.description: Cost center associated with the target.

orcl_gtp_location.description: Physical or logical location of the target.

associations.description: List of associations linking this target to other targets, such as cluster membership or composite relationships.

canonicalLink.description: Canonical REST API URI for accessing this target resource.
